question.text: Which of the following statements about an Authorised Person (AP) under SEBI (Stock Brokers) Regulations is CORRECT?

answer.text: An AP can be appointed by multiple Trading Members simultaneously
An AP can directly receive securities and funds from clients in its own name
All acts of omissions and commission of the AP shall be deemed to be that of the Trading Member
An AP can charge fees directly to clients for services rendered

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The regulations clearly state that all acts of omissions and commission of the Authorised Person shall be deemed to be that of the Trading Member. The Trading Member is responsible for all acts of the AP. **The Trap:** Option about receiving securities/funds is tempting because APs do interact with clients, but the regulations explicitly state that an AP shall not receive or pay any money or securities in its own name or account. **Cross-Exam Context:** The principal-agent relationship between Trading Members and APs is a key concept in NISM Series III-A and VIII exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A person cannot be appointed as an AP by more than one Trading Member, and a partner/director of an AP cannot separately be an AP with the exchange.
